rules.when: many independent points, literature lists, or dense explanations
rules.guidance: Use a text-first composition with clear hierarchy; do not force sparse cards.

rules.when: causal, procedural, temporal, or methodological relationships are central
rules.guidance: Turn the relationship into a flow, timeline, or simple mechanism diagram.

rules.when: one figure, image, or table carries the evidence
rules.guidance: Let the exhibit dominate and keep interpretation notes concise.

rules.when: parallel categories, risks, contributions, or suggestions need comparison
rules.guidance: Use cards only when the source content is genuinely parallel.

variants.best_for: default text, mixed text/image, or custom figure composition
variants.layout: one open content canvas inside CONTENT_AREA; compose content according to the slide claim

variants.best_for: three parallel findings, needs, contributions, risks, or feasibility points
variants.layout: optional three solid rounded cards generated inside CONTENT_AREA, not fixed in the base SVG shell

variants.best_for: one dominant figure with concise interpretation notes
variants.layout: figure occupies 58-68% of CONTENT_AREA; notes sit in a right or bottom annotation band
variants.image_policy: preserve aspect ratio; crop only when the user requests it

variants.best_for: domestic versus foreign research, before/after, method comparison, or ablation explanation
variants.layout: two balanced columns with shared title rows and one short synthesis line

variants.best_for: one evidence figure plus methods, observations, or implications
variants.layout: left figure block, right structured text or callout stack

variants.best_for: technical route, workflow, experiment pipeline, or schedule
variants.layout: 3-5 horizontal or vertical steps using primary blue labels and light gray connectors

variants.best_for: research content, work plan, innovation comparison, feasibility matrix
variants.layout: compact matrix with a primary-blue header row and readable body text
variants.constraint: table text should stay >=14px; split dense tables

variants.best_for: four research questions, metrics, modules, or contribution blocks
variants.layout: 2x2 grid inside CONTENT_AREA with consistent gutters and restrained blue headers
